provenance.source_corpus: Early recruitment of boreal forest trees in hybrid poplar plantations of different densities on mine waste rock slopes

source_spans.text: Density effects of young hybrid poplars were assessed on the emergence and survival of early, mid and late successional species
Results were also compared in 2 × 2-m plantations with and without a hydroseeded herbaceous cover
values measured during the period of maximal biomass decreased linearly with tree density
linear regression coefficients indicated a significant positive effect of poplar density on soil moisture conditions
The 1 × 1-m poplar cover intercepted on average ~70% of full sunlight
soil temperature was the lowest under the 1 × 1-m spacing treatment
seedling emergence of naturally established Salicaceae (Populus and Salix) species followed a quadratic pattern along the density gradient
summer mortality rates... increased linearly with hybrid poplar density in 2015
rates increased linearly with poplar density for both species
rates increased linearly with poplar density for both species
The positive effect of density on recruitment success after one growing season was significant only for A. balsamea
significant differences were observed under the hydroseeded treatment... with higher rates registered in hydroeseeded plots
